f354322a-8236-41a3-bcf2-1d6813c307a0.issue_description: The mesh node connection drops frequently. I need to know what channel width to set.
f354322a-8236-41a3-bcf2-1d6813c307a0.latest_question: What exact behavior do you observe right before the issue happens?
probable_causes:
probable_causes: ["Adjacent channel interference", "Outdated router firmware", "Mesh nodes placed too close to metal obstructions"],
follow_up_question: "What is the current channel width setting for your Moss Router X1?",
next_step: "Access the router's administrator console to adjust the channel width.",
recommended_action: "Change the channel width from 80MHz to 40MHz or 20MHz to reduce adjacent channel interference. Additionally, ensure mesh nodes are placed at least 15 feet away from metal obstructions and check for firmware updates."
f354322a-8236-41a3-bcf2-1d6813c307a0.next_step: Collect one more symptom and compare it against the referenced documentation.
f354322a-8236-41a3-bcf2-1d6813c307a0.recommended_action: Review the retrieved product documentation before taking corrective action.

c6b79cdc-cb0e-4099-9b1a-2b841315fb09.issue_description: The mesh node connection drops frequently. I need to know what channel width to set.
c6b79cdc-cb0e-4099-9b1a-2b841315fb09.latest_question: Have you checked if the router's firmware is up to date, or if there are any metal obstructions within 15 feet of the mesh nodes?
c6b79cdc-cb0e-4099-9b1a-2b841315fb09.next_step: Log in to the router's administrator console and navigate to the wireless settings to change the channel width.
c6b79cdc-cb0e-4099-9b1a-2b841315fb09.recommended_action: Change the channel width from 80MHz to 40MHz or 20MHz to reduce adjacent channel interference.

f5b78c3f-7531-443d-b095-fac126289082.issue_description: My mesh node won't connect to the main router
f5b78c3f-7531-443d-b095-fac126289082.latest_question: What is the status light on the mesh node doing?
probable_causes: The mesh node is not in close enough proximity to the main router for initial pairing.
There is a hardware synchronization failure between the mesh node and the main router.
The mesh node and main router have mismatched firmware versions.
f5b78c3f-7531-443d-b095-fac126289082.next_step: Bring the mesh node to the same room as the main router, within 5-10 feet, to ensure optimal conditions for pairing.
f5b78c3f-7531-443d-b095-fac126289082.recommended_action: Perform a factory reset on the mesh node by holding the reset button for 10 seconds, then attempt the pairing process again.

ed1055d8-db1b-4541-939b-a2f0fa711b6c.issue_description: Router keeps disconnecting every few minutes
ed1055d8-db1b-4541-939b-a2f0fa711b6c.latest_question: Does the router's status light pulse amber?
ed1055d8-db1b-4541-939b-a2f0fa711b6c.recommended_action: Log in to the router admin portal (http://192.168.1.1), navigate to Wireless Settings, and change the 2.4GHz channel to 1, 6, or 11, and the 5GHz channel to 36, 44, or 149. Additionally, reduce the channel width from 80MHz to 40MHz or 20MHz.

07e39f97-832b-4be6-a8e1-99f487caa4a1.latest_question: Is the amber light blinking on the main router or a satellite mesh node?
probable_causes: Lost connection to the main unit (if it is a satellite mesh node)
07e39f97-832b-4be6-a8e1-99f487caa4a1.next_step: Identify the type of unit (main router or satellite node) that is blinking amber to determine the specific cause and resolution.
07e39f97-832b-4be6-a8e1-99f487caa4a1.recommended_action: If the blinking unit is a satellite mesh node, move it closer to the main router to re-establish connection.

88968a50-517c-4bc4-8a8d-330a8c266365.issue_description: How do I add another mesh node?
88968a50-517c-4bc4-8a8d-330a8c266365.latest_question: Have you already powered on the secondary node, and if so, what is the current status of its LED indicator?
probable_causes: User is unfamiliar with the procedure for adding a secondary mesh node to the Moss Router X1 system.
88968a50-517c-4bc4-8a8d-330a8c266365.next_step: Follow the wireless node pairing procedure outlined in the product documentation.
88968a50-517c-4bc4-8a8d-330a8c266365.recommended_action: To add a secondary node, first place it in the same room as the main Moss Router X1, within 5-10 feet. Power on the secondary node and wait for its LED to turn solid amber, then pulse amber slowly. In the mobile app, tap 'Add Mesh Node'. Press the sync button on the back of the main router for 3 seconds until its LED pulses blue. Next, press the sync button on the secondary node. The nodes will pair, and the secondary node LED will flash green to confirm pairing.

832bf5f2-6091-43c4-8788-f2cfd1f084b5.latest_question: Are you looking to enable automatic updates or perform a manual update?
probable_causes: User is seeking general instructions on how to update firmware.
User wants to ensure their device is running the latest firmware version.
832bf5f2-6091-43c4-8788-f2cfd1f084b5.next_step: Based on your preference, we can provide detailed instructions for either enabling automatic updates or performing a manual firmware update.
832bf5f2-6091-43c4-8788-f2cfd1f084b5.recommended_action: The Moss Router X1 supports automatic over-the-air (OTA) updates by default, which check for new firmware every night at 3:00 AM. Alternatively, you can perform a manual update by downloading the firmware file and uploading it via the router's web admin portal.

993aa596-ae8b-4761-a061-e132595c227d.latest_question: What is the color and state (solid, blinking, off) of the LED status light on the front panel of the main router unit?
probable_causes: The Moss Router X1 may not be correctly connected to your modem.
The router may not have successfully established an internet connection.
A Double NAT configuration might be preventing proper network functionality if connected to an ISP gateway.
993aa596-ae8b-4761-a061-e132595c227d.next_step: Verify that the Ethernet cable is securely connected between the blue Internet port (WAN) of the Moss Router X1 and your modem's LAN port.
993aa596-ae8b-4761-a061-e132595c227d.recommended_action: Ensure all physical connections, especially the Ethernet cable between the router and modem, are secure and clicked into place.

b7e0513b-99a5-4fb5-86c8-1aca5ac8de5a.latest_question: What color is the LED status light on the front panel of the main router unit?
probable_causes: Incorrect or loose physical connection between the router and the modem.
The router has not successfully connected to the Internet.
Hardware synchronization failure or outdated firmware.
b7e0513b-99a5-4fb5-86c8-1aca5ac8de5a.next_step: Ensure the Ethernet cable connecting the blue Internet port (WAN) of the Moss Router X1 to your modem's LAN port is securely connected.
b7e0513b-99a5-4fb5-86c8-1aca5ac8de5a.recommended_action: Check the physical connections between the router and the modem.

13c31a4b-3e21-4545-97dc-9e5b4afdac2f.latest_question: Is the router experiencing any functional problems, such as connectivity issues or unusual LED indicators?
probable_causes: The provided documentation does not address cosmetic issues such as faded print on the device.
13c31a4b-3e21-4545-97dc-9e5b4afdac2f.next_step: If the router is experiencing functional problems, please describe them in detail. If the issue is purely cosmetic, it is not covered by the available troubleshooting guides.
13c31a4b-3e21-4545-97dc-9e5b4afdac2f.recommended_action: No specific action can be recommended based on the provided documentation for cosmetic issues.

60b0c44d-1a67-4c23-8f04-847d2e62ec7d.latest_question: What is the current color and state (solid, blinking, off) of the LED status light on the front panel of your Moss Router X1?
probable_causes: Incorrect or loose physical connection between the Moss Router X1 and your modem.
No active internet connection from your Internet Service Provider (ISP) or modem.
Double NAT configuration, which can prevent proper network operation.
60b0c44d-1a67-4c23-8f04-847d2e62ec7d.next_step: Ensure the Ethernet cable connecting the blue Internet port (WAN) of the Moss Router X1 to your modem's LAN port is securely connected and clicks into place.
60b0c44d-1a67-4c23-8f04-847d2e62ec7d.recommended_action: Perform a factory reset on your Moss Router X1 by pressing and holding the pinhole reset button on the rear panel for 10 seconds while the unit is powered on. Wait 2 minutes for the router to boot with factory defaults.

ed785cfc-8223-4d6c-84e9-27cb9ff0d78b.latest_question: What is the color and state (solid, blinking, pulsing) of the LED status light on the front panel of your Moss Router X1?
probable_causes: Loose or incorrect Ethernet cable connection between the router and modem.
No internet signal being received by the Moss Router X1 from your modem/ISP.
ed785cfc-8223-4d6c-84e9-27cb9ff0d78b.next_step: Check the physical Ethernet cable connection between the Moss Router X1's blue Internet (WAN) port and your modem's LAN port, ensuring it is secure and clicks in place.
ed785cfc-8223-4d6c-84e9-27cb9ff0d78b.recommended_action: Perform a power cycle of both your modem and the Moss Router X1. Unplug both devices from power, wait 30 seconds, then plug in the modem first. Once the modem is fully online, plug in the Moss Router X1.

probable_causes: Incompatibility with legacy Wi-Fi 7 client devices

910e9875-195b-4208-b11e-7bbd688b03db.issue_description: My mesh node won't connect to the main router
910e9875-195b-4208-b11e-7bbd688b03db.latest_question: What color is the status light on the secondary node, and is it pulsing or solid?
probable_causes: Node placed too far from the main router during pairing

23491a9e-53a8-4b2c-84e1-b1d20b9f8995.issue_description:
I need a demo-ready version of my application. The real product upload/backend flow is currently not working, so I want you to temporarily replace it with realistic fake data for a polished demo.

Please analyze my codebase and make the following changes:

1. Fake AI Product Upload Flow
Do not call the real API/backend.
When the user uploads a product image and clicks the upload button, simulate an AI analysis process.
Show realistic loading states such as:
"Uploading image..."
"Analyzing product with AI..."
"Generating marketplace data..."
Add a 2–3 second delay to make the AI processing feel realistic.

After the loading finishes, automatically return a realistic product analysis, for example:

Product Name: Nike Air Max 270
Brand: Nike
Category: Running Shoes
Estimated Price: $120
AI Confidence: 98%
Description: "AI successfully identified the product and generated optimized marketplace information."
2. Make the UI Look Real
Display the uploaded image as if it was analyzed.
Add smooth animations, progress indicators, success checkmarks, and professional transitions.
Make it look like a real AI system is processing the product.
3. Add Demo Marketplace Data

Create 8–10 realistic sample products with:

Product image
Product name
Brand
Price
Rating
Short description
Category

Make the marketplace look populated and production-ready.

4. Keep the Original Code Safe

Do not delete the original API implementation.
Instead:

Add a clear DEMO_MODE flag or separate mock data file.
Structure the code so I can switch back to the real backend later by changing a single variable.
5. Maintain Existing Design
Keep my current design language, colors, and components.
Do not redesign the entire app.
Only improve the user experience around the demo flow.
6. Provide Complete Code Changes

For every file you modify:

Tell me the exact file path.
Explain why it was changed.
Provide the complete updated file code, not snippets.

My goal is to record a hackathon demo, so the application should appear fully functional even though the backend is mocked.
23491a9e-53a8-4b2c-84e1-b1d20b9f8995.latest_question: Are you using a specific frontend framework like React or Vue, and where is your current API service file located?

Lack of conditional logic for demo vs. production environments
23491a9e-53a8-4b2c-84e1-b1d20b9f8995.next_step: Create a 'config.js' file to define a 'DEMO_MODE' constant and a 'mockData.js' file to store your product array.
23491a9e-53a8-4b2c-84e1-b1d20b9f8995.recommended_action: Implement a conditional check in your upload function that toggles between the real API call and a 'setTimeout' promise that resolves with your mock product object when 'DEMO_MODE' is true.

ee1f1417-49ab-441a-9625-19c59372b1ba.issue_description: My hhh has a blue blinking light, what is the cause?
ee1f1417-49ab-441a-9625-19c59372b1ba.latest_question: Is the unit currently placed in an enclosed space, or is it near a heat source like a radiator or direct sunlight?
ee1f1417-49ab-441a-9625-19c59372b1ba.next_step: Relocate the device to a cooler, well-ventilated area.
ee1f1417-49ab-441a-9625-19c59372b1ba.recommended_action: Move the hhh unit to a cooler area to allow the CPU temperature to normalize.
ee1f1417-49ab-441a-9625-19c59372b1ba.investigation_reasoning: The blue blinking light on your hhh unit is explicitly identified in the product manual as an E9 error code, which indicates that the internal CPU is overheating. My diagnostic process focuses on thermal management, as this error suggests the device is unable to dissipate heat effectively in its current environment.

3e26494f-1e97-42a3-9cf1-66642752763e.issue_description: What does a green blinking light on hhh mean?
3e26494f-1e97-42a3-9cf1-66642752763e.latest_question: After performing a restart, does the light return to a solid state, or does it continue to blink?
3e26494f-1e97-42a3-9cf1-66642752763e.recommended_action: Restart the product to clear the low memory error.
3e26494f-1e97-42a3-9cf1-66642752763e.investigation_reasoning: According to the hhh product manual, a green blinking light corresponds to error code E10, which indicates that the device is experiencing a low memory state. This is a common operational status that typically requires a simple power cycle to clear the system cache and restore normal functionality.

06cc5197-4624-4d17-ade7-d62585f034e2.issue_description: I have a green blinking light on my device hhh. What should I do?
06cc5197-4624-4d17-ade7-d62585f034e2.latest_question: After performing the restart, does the light return to a solid state or does it continue to blink?
06cc5197-4624-4d17-ade7-d62585f034e2.recommended_action: Restart the product by unplugging it from the power source for 30 seconds and plugging it back in.
06cc5197-4624-4d17-ade7-d62585f034e2.investigation_reasoning: The green blinking light on the hhh device corresponds to error code E10, which is explicitly documented as a low memory state. My diagnostic process focuses on clearing the current volatile memory state to restore normal operation, as this is the standard recovery procedure for this specific error code.
